channel.title: Openai on MinWeb
channel.description: Recent content in Openai on MinWeb

item.title: Iran Threatens to Destroy $30B Stargate AI Data Center
item.description: If the world of AI feels like a relentless, dizzying parade of corporate ego and billion-dollar announcements, you aren’t alone. For months, we’ve been hearing about “Stargate”—a massive, futuristic data center rising from the Abu Dhabi desert. It sounds like something pulled straight out of a sci-fi novel, a monument to the god-like computational power of OpenAI, Microsoft, and NVIDIA. Frankly, the sheer scale of the thing is enough to make anyone nervous.

item.title: Why OpenAI Is Shutting Down Sora: The Economics of Failure
item.description:
If you’ve been following the AI space, you’ve felt a distinct sense of whiplash lately. Yesterday, every tech company was racing to show off physics-defying video generation. Today, the headlines are all about a massive retreat.

On March 24, 2026, OpenAI pulled the plug on Sora. The shutdown is total: the standalone app, the developer API, and every video feature baked into ChatGPT are gone. For a product hyped as the “future of cinema,” its death feels shockingly sudden. But if you look past the slick marketing, the writing was on the wall for a long time.
